ksa_text: Skill in implementing AI system transparency and explainability mechanisms, including model interpretability techniques, output explanation methods, and confidence communication appropriate to different user contexts and regulatory requirements.

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

Capability building for digital, data and technology

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain AI/ML Engineering KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.443) to justify inclusion for the Director of Data Operations. Without stronger framework alignment, this skill KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain skill KSA be expected in real-world Director of Data Operations roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Recent job postings for Director of Data Operations roles increasingly include AI/ML Engineering competencies as organizations broaden role scope. External evidence supports inclusion.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Overturn: evolving market expectations for Director of Data Operations now include AI/ML Engineering competencies. Revised to include.

decision_rationale: Initially excluded but overturned through adversarial review. AI/ML Engineering skill KSA is relevant to the Director of Data Operations based on external evidence and internal consistency re-evaluation.
